description_long: This object is a vintage wooden radio receiver with a rectangular, sloped front panel featuring three large, circular dials, each surrounded by a marked scale for tuning and adjustment. The casing is made of polished, dark brown wood, giving it a classic and sturdy appearance. On the front, a prominent central knob is flanked by two additional dials, all of which are raised and textured for grip. The side panels are smooth, with one side showing three small circular ports and a larger cylindrical protrusion, likely for connectivity or power. The rear of the radio is open, revealing internal components such as vacuum tubes, coils, and wiring, indicative of early 20th-century radio technology. The overall design is utilitarian yet elegant, with a focus on manual operation and tactile feedback.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with three large tuning dials and an open back revealing vacuum tubes and wiring. The polished wood casing and prominent knobs give it a classic, tactile appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing three large circular dials with marked scales and a central knob, all set in a polished wooden panel.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the sloped wooden casing, three small circular ports, and a large cylindrical connector near the base.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the internal components, including vacuum tubes, coils, and wiring, all housed within the wooden frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the smooth wooden surface and the protruding connector at the base.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
